L2.description: A template injection vulnerability allows attackers to inject arbitrary Java Expression Language (EL) expressions, leading to unauthenticated Remote Code Execution (RCE). User-controlled input is passed unsanitized into a message template that evaluates embedded EL expressions at runtime. This enables an attacker to execute arbitrary code on the server by crafting malicious input strings containing EL payloads.

L3.description: A template injection vulnerability allows attackers to inject arbitrary Java Expression Language (EL) expressions, leading to unauthenticated Remote Code Execution (RCE). User-controlled input is passed unsanitized into a message template that evaluates embedded EL expressions at runtime. This enables an attacker to execute arbitrary code on the server by crafting malicious input strings containing EL payloads.

ground_truth.reason: User-controlled cron expression input is passed via exception getMessage() into a Bean Validation constraint violation message template, which evaluates embedded Java EL expressions, enabling arbitrary remote code execution.
